[2018-05-18 23:08] kdansky: So question: When you have 3g left over in the midgame, do you still buy a drone?
[2018-05-18 23:09] kdansky: similar for engi with 2, though there I find I just buy engies when I need 1 hp blocking for granularity
[2018-05-18 23:09] wiwiweb: engineer is usually a good mid-game buy
[2018-05-18 23:09] kdansky: but since they do nothing, generally no
[2018-05-18 23:10] kdansky: but if I have 3g and 1energy over, I might as well buy a drone. if it works *once*, I break even for blocking. if it works twice, Iwin
[2018-05-18 23:10] kdansky: though I do lose out on 1g in the bank
[2018-05-18 23:12] jiecut: I think the working paying off depends on inflation.

[2018-05-18 23:18] wiwiweb: if you have to block for 1 next turn, an engineer cost you 2 where a drone cost you 3

[2018-05-19 21:06] kdansky: So, theoretical question: Why is Isochronus cheaper than Gauss Cannon (and why does that match my experience with the unit?) - it deals the same damage over time, and the higher fluctuation is more likely to result in a breach, or painful destruction of expensive absorbers. Why is it that in theory, Iso should be better, but in practise, it's quite a bit worse?
[2018-05-19 21:07] f300xen (f0x): gauss cannon does 1->2->3->4 damage but iso does 0->2->2->4 damage total
[2018-05-19 21:08] liadahlia (Lia): Iso delays 1 damage by 1 turn
[2018-05-19 21:08] liadahlia (Lia): Which is bad the same reason tarsiers are bad
[2018-05-19 21:08] wilson0862 (Wilson): Syncing is overrated. Damage earlier is generally better
[2018-05-19 21:09] f300xen (f0x): iso is slightly better as first attacker iirc
[2018-05-19 21:09] liadahlia (Lia): Although in my opinion iso is often better than goose at least a little bit
[2018-05-19 21:10] liadahlia (Lia): If it's able to deny absorb every other turn
[2018-05-19 21:13] p0lari (Polari): iso can be good as a first attacker the same way as tarsier
[2018-05-19 21:14] p0lari (Polari): if you assume the first damage would just get absorbed, the comparison with goose is 0-1-2-3 vs 0-2-2-4
[2018-05-19 21:14] .bky_1556 (bky): If the first cannon shot would be absorbed you can float 6G
[2018-05-19 21:14] .bky_1556 (bky): Usually you have something better to do than float it
[2018-05-19 21:15] p0lari (Polari): and if this is a high econ game where you want to buy four green attackers at once as your first?
[2018-05-19 21:18] p0lari (Polari): also even if you did get to buy an extra drone or something by delaying the goose purchase, iso is still one gold cheaper so you need that much value there just to break even
[2018-05-19 21:19] kdansky: yeah, my last game I bought them basically because they were cheaper than Gauss
[2018-05-19 21:19] kdansky: And I had greens lying around and no other tech that wasn't used for more important stuff (like big expensive blue units)
[2018-05-19 21:20] kdansky: And my opponent went for Gauss instead, and I handily lost
[2018-05-19 21:20] kdansky: but it was probably not the gausses that did me in, but the fact that he had a bunch of Tarsiers putting out pressure, and I did not.

[2018-05-19 21:31] .bky_1556 (bky): Synched Isos are better vs. large absorbers.
[2018-05-19 21:33] .bky_1556 (bky): The first couple prod the opponent into sinking the absorber's cost, then it only gets to absorb on one of the three following turns.
[2018-05-19 21:39] Deleted User (Deleted User): >it deals the same damage over time, and the higher fluctuation is more likely to result in a breach

both of those are untrue
[2018-05-19 21:41] darkeisbein: higher fluctuation of attack does not make breaching easier because your opponent still has the option to buy soak as they would need it for less fluctuating attack
[2018-05-19 21:41] darkeisbein: they just also get the additional option of buying slow soak or attackers

[2018-05-19 21:54] swapgo: Theyre good if theres bad Soak, because you deny absorb
[2018-05-19 21:54] swapgo: Also costing 1G less makes a difference
[2018-05-19 21:55] swapgo: Like if theres stuff like Xeno, Penetrators, Lifespan stuff or Aegis n such, then Iso is a bad purchase
[2018-05-19 21:56] swapgo: But if there are good Drone alternatives and bad Soak then you can get aggro with Isos
[2018-05-19 21:58] darkeisbein: I mean if you only have synced up isos then there is some value to it yes
[2018-05-19 21:59] darkeisbein: but if you have isos on top of regular attackers they are never better then gausscannons, they are at best just as good (aside from the fact that they are cheaper)
[2018-05-19 22:00] darkeisbein: (and some very niche situations with lifespan soak)
[2018-05-19 22:00] swapgo: Yeah that also helps isos
[2018-05-19 22:00] swapgo: Good alternatives to constant damage
[2018-05-19 22:04] .bky_1556 (bky): Niche situations with lifespan soak? It goes deeper than that. e.g. hitting  a frontline breakpoint exactly.
[2018-05-19 22:05] xyotsuba: yeah frontline and lifespan change how good periodic damage is
[2018-05-19 22:05] xyotsuba: but unlike with scorch and imolite you cant hold isos
[2018-05-19 22:05] xyotsuba: so the opponent can exploit your iso sync with their frontline and lifespan units just as easily as u can exploit them
[2018-05-19 22:06] xyotsuba: and actually it is easier for them to do it because they know when u will build ur next isos
[2018-05-19 22:07] .bky_1556 (bky): If your opponent soaks the extra iso damage by prebuying prompt defense, they're better than gausses.

--- Thread (41 messages, 2018-05-19) ---
[2018-05-19 22:42] miccull. (Miccull): Hey guys , what should i do to improve at prismata ? I started 2 days ago and so far im doing the Campaign to learn the game.
[2018-05-19 22:43] redzonex (Redzone): Once you do chapter 1, do the Combat Training
[2018-05-19 22:44] redzonex (Redzone): up to I'd say when you complete all the advanced challenges
[2018-05-19 22:44] redzonex (Redzone): then try playing games against bots, find the level of bot you can't easily beat, and practice playing against it until you can
[2018-05-19 22:45] redzonex (Redzone): do that until you can easily beat masterbot and then go stomp people on ranked :LongNoseLiar:
[2018-05-19 22:45] crash_overlord: Or smash your head into the keyboard and win :clordSmart:
[2018-05-19 22:46] leary93: I mean, just rush animus for tarsiers, Ive heard they are the most broken unit in the game
[2018-05-19 22:46] miccull. (Miccull): tarsiers ? i find them to slow for my taste lol
[2018-05-19 22:47] darkeisbein: tarsiers are the best attackers in the base set
[2018-05-19 22:48] leary93: It's just a meme, a while back there was someone who complained about them being broken, and we tried to actively help the guy improve, but he was too stubborn and kept calling tarsier unbeatable
[2018-05-19 22:48] leary93: So we said: why dont u just try do tarsier strats yourself instead and see its weaknesses
[2018-05-19 22:48] darkeisbein: building tarsiers is a pretty legit strat tho
[2018-05-19 22:49] leary93: It's definetly not always a legit strat when bonus units are involved
[2018-05-19 22:49] Deleted User (Deleted User): why would someone call a 5th percentile strategy good
[2018-05-19 22:49] Deleted User (Deleted User): that shit just boggles my mind
[2018-05-19 22:49] redzonex (Redzone): and new players suck at evaluating strategies
[2018-05-19 22:50] redzonex (Redzone): also a lot of times new players will see these big expensive units and do awkward build orders to get up to then
[2018-05-19 22:50] leary93: but its not the fact they're new that they're ignorant to help
[2018-05-19 22:50] leary93: that's just being ignorant xD
[2018-05-19 22:50] redzonex (Redzone): and then just get rolled by BSO WTT every turn
[2018-05-19 22:50] leary93: and this guy totally didn't understand we we're actively trying to help him
[2018-05-19 22:51] redzonex (Redzone): yeah I'm with you, being stubborn and ignorant is a pita
[2018-05-19 22:51] darkeisbein: especially against new players you can do really well with tarsiers though because they often econ up way to much
[2018-05-19 22:51] leary93: anyways @Miccull there's multiple ways to learn, a good start is just doing pve content (missions, combat training and bots) and then transition into pvp games (either casual or ranked, depending on what you're comfortable with)
[2018-05-19 22:52] leary93: And when playing pvp, try to learn what your opponent does, if you find something hard to play against, try to play it yourself and see the weakness
[2018-05-19 22:52] leary93: dont get blinded on having to progress fast, as it's a lot more valueable learning from the games you do play
[2018-05-19 22:53] leary93: also, 307th's blog.prismata.net is superuseful
[2018-05-19 22:54] leary93: https://prismatalibrary.blog/
The Prismata Library
[2018-05-19 22:54] leary93: that is the one i mean xD
[2018-05-19 22:55] miccull. (Miccull): i have background as a chess player , but i find this game much harder lol. thanks for the help guys
[2018-05-19 22:57] miccull. (Miccull): btw how do you find the game ? are there any units that you have to rush or it is somewhat balanced ?
[2018-05-19 22:58] redzonex (Redzone): the game is very well balanced tbh
[2018-05-19 22:59] redzonex (Redzone): p1/p2 winrates overall are roughly 48%/52%, and iirc no one unit swings that more than 3% in either direction
[2018-05-19 22:59] redzonex (Redzone): some units are stronger or more impactful than others but every unit has a niche where it's very good
[2018-05-19 23:07] miccull. (Miccull): Ok , i understand . Any standard starting strategies ? by standard i mean that can be used in most of the situation. i consider myself smart enough to adjust it to the current game
[2018-05-19 23:08] darkeisbein: https://prismatalibrary.blog/new-start-here/
New? Start Here
This is a blog about teaching Prismata strategy. A good place to start is with The Standard Style. You can also check out Common Openings to learn some good builds. After you’ve read those tw…
[2018-05-19 23:08] darkeisbein: both the standard style and common openings kinda discuss that
[2018-05-19 23:08] redzonex (Redzone): https://prismatalibrary.blog/2017/12/22/the-standard-style/
The Standard Style
This guide will teach you the standard style of play – a style of play that is simple enough for a beginner to be able to learn, while still being grounded in real high level Prismata strateg…
[2018-05-19 23:09] miccull. (Miccull): thanks guys , really appreciate the help
[2018-05-19 23:17] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): btw you may want to update your description of lucina on this article https://prismatalibrary.blog/2018/02/22/big-red-units-amporilla-vai-tatsu-lucina-shadowfang/

[2018-05-22 16:31] inasyn: What is the point of ossified dronea, except breach protection?
[2018-05-22 16:38] unearthly: Does it need additional points?
[2018-05-22 16:39] p0lari (Polari): if you're going to hold drones next turn anyway, it's similar to buying an engi for soak
[2018-05-22 16:39] p0lari (Polari): spend an extra red, get a gold back next turn
[2018-05-22 16:40] p0lari (Polari): in some games you use it as a cheap 2hp absorber early
[2018-05-22 16:40] supergreyman (Greyman): It's like buying a Forcefield in advance, but costs wayyy less.
[2018-05-22 16:40] p0lari (Polari): and in games where you have extra red otherwise, spending it on turning your drones into vigilant 2hp soak is *really good*
[2018-05-22 16:40] medar: Especially if red is hard to spend otherwise, which is common towards the end of the game
[2018-05-22 16:42] medar: Sometimes it also has a purpose to absorb for one. But of course playing around threat is the main strength.
[2018-05-22 16:43] p0lari (Polari): red being cheap late doesn't help as much as you'd think
[2018-05-22 16:44] p0lari (Polari): because to keep clicking one and blocking with one you need to have bought it two turns earlier
[2018-05-22 16:44] p0lari (Polari): to keep clicking two and blocking with two, three turns earlier
[2018-05-22 16:44] p0lari (Polari): and usually by the time you have stopped caring about using red for rhinos or whatever you need defence right now
[2018-05-22 16:45] p0lari (Polari): I mean using it like that can still be good but it's not as easy as just oh I no longer need red otherwise so I'll just dump it into ossi
[2018-05-22 16:47] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, thinking a few turns ahead is the hard part.
